message: agent2: implementing deep-merge behavior in Features.update() in src/datasets/features/features.py. Will touch the update() method only. Let me know if you're also touching that file.

agent1: ack. I'm adding a keep_features_dicts_synced decorator to Features class in features.py that wraps __setitem__, __delitem__, update, etc to sync _column_requires_decoding. This will touch update() too (wrapping it). Let's coordinate: I'll wrap the existing update() with the decorator rather than rewriting its body, so your deep-merge logic inside update() should still work fine as long as I only wrap it, not rewrite it. I'll ping you when I push my change so you can rebase your update() body on top.

agent2: done with deep-merge in update(). My update() still has the same signature update(self, iterable, **kwds) and internally calls self[key] = ... for every key (never mutates dict via other means), so wrapping it with a decorator that recomputes _column_requires_decoding after the call should be totally fine - no conflicts expected. I also unwrap Sequence.feature dicts for merging nested Sequence features. Will merge your branch before final patch. Ping me when pushed.
